reasoning:
On Wikipedia, FIDE ratings are updated monthly. As of April 2025, Fabiano Caruana's listed rating is 2776. To achieve a 1% increase over this April baseline, he would need to gain approximately 28 Elo points, reaching ~2804. Gaining 28 points at the very top of classical chess requires a monumental and prolonged performance, practically requiring an individual to go undefeated and win several games against world-class opponents in high-stakes classical tournaments.

For the May dates (May 4 and May 27), the official FIDE May list will be in effect. Recent live ratings in late April show no realistic path for him to have made a 28-point leap in a single unrecorded month, keeping the probability extremely low and bounded at the strict minimum of 0.05. By July (3 months horizon), he will have participated in more events, allowing a slight chance of achieving a hot streak, moving the probability to 0.10. By October (6 months horizon), Caruana could potentially put together a run that returns him to the >2800 rating range, which he had successfully maintained in early 2024, placing my long-term estimate around 0.15. Uncertainty structurally widens into the future because of the longer window for extreme variance in performance, but the fundamental obstacle of securing 28 Elo points anchors the odds low.
